_comment: Seeded hardware-tier profiles. RESOLVED 2026-06-14 (see Ayre_Project_Plan.md 'Hardware tiers'). All values are TUNABLE config, not code literals. Every tier/threshold carries a 'rationale' so a tester can see WHY their machine landed where it did and revise with real data. Cut points are stored ABSOLUTE and do NOT auto-adjust; loading a model whose footprint differs from these assumptions must raise a review/recalculate DIALOGUE (user-control), not change silently. REFRAMED 2026-06-23: tiers shifted up one -- the 8GB laptop is now MID (Qwen3-8B), the 16GB desktop is HIGH (Qwen3-30B-A3B offload), and LOW is a FUTURE sub-8GB target. EXTENDED 2026-06-23: split the old open-ended HIGH band into HIGH [16,24) = 30B offload (desktop validated), ULTRA [24,32) = 30B fully resident + concurrent-agent headroom (seed, not validated), and a new top tier MAX [32,inf) = bigger-than-30B / workstation (FUTURE, designed-not-built, no hardware to validate yet). Bands are half-open [lo, hi): a card matches when vram >= lo and vram < hi (hi=null means open-ended). NOTE 2026-06-24: default_model removed from all tiers -- Auto now uses _pick_best_fitting_model() in config.py (largest GGUF that fits without disk-thrash).

axis.rationale: VRAM is the gradient. RAM is a veto/downgrade check that enforces the hard floor but is not its own gradient. CPU affects speed not feasibility (moot under effectiveness-over-speed).

floor.rationale: Not a fixed VRAM number. The floor is reached when VRAM+RAM together cannot hold weights + KV + compute buffers + OS overhead. Below it: warn and recommend a smaller model; never pretend to run.

fixed_toggles_all_tiers._note: All user-overridable. Web is greyed out while Agents is OFF (dependent-toggle rule).

low.status: FUTURE / designed -- no sub-8GB hardware validated yet
low.meaning: lighter hardware than the 8GB baseline; the 'improve to work on low tier later' target

context.rationale: Provisional ~16-32K @ q8_0; settle against the chosen small model's GGUF when built.

reranker_placement.rationale: Smallest reranker on CPU on-demand so it never competes for the very tight VRAM budget of sub-8GB hardware.

low._verify: Tier not yet validated on hardware; all values provisional.

mid.meaning: VALIDATED BASELINE (laptop): chat model fully GPU-resident, conservative defaults
mid.validated_on: laptop RTX 3070 Ti Laptop, 8GB VRAM / 16GB RAM

context.rationale: 32K @ q8_0; verify against GGUF, fall back to 16K if tight.

reranker_placement.rationale: 8GB VRAM is fully consumed by the GPU-resident chat model + KV; the 278M reranker (~0.3GB) stays on CPU and loads only during retrieval, so it never competes for the tight VRAM budget. CPU rerank latency is acceptable under effectiveness-over-speed. User-overridable in Settings.

mid._verify: KV figures are computed estimates; VERIFY against actual GGUF metadata on the dev box.

high.meaning: the 30B OFFLOAD tier: ~70/30 CPU offload (desktop validated at 16GB); the offload path the whole design centers on

context.rationale: 64K @ q8_0 default at 16GB doubles the working window vs 32K at the same ~3GB KV cost (so the ~70/30 split holds), low-risk 2x YaRN. 128K available as a user override (slow / may degrade under offload). Per-token KV ~96KB @ FP16 (~48 layers, GQA ~4 KV heads, head_dim 128) -- VERIFY vs GGUF.

offload_split_seed.rationale: SEED the known-good dev layer count here once measured (current ~70/30 split on the 16GB desktop). Used to get an end-to-end skeleton running before the real auto-detector exists. null until filled from the dev box.

ultra.status: SEED / designed -- 24-32GB residency not validated on hardware yet
ultra.meaning: the 30B RESIDENT tier: the same 30B-A3B fully GPU-resident (no offload spill), comfortable big context, and the band where concurrent agents activate once built
ultra.validated_on: none yet -- 24GB+ residency was a seed inside the old open-ended HIGH band; promoted to its own tier 2026-06-23

context.rationale: 128K @ q8_0 is comfortable + resident at 24GB+ (4x YaRN caveat -- may degrade; user-overridable). Falls back to 64K if KV is tighter than estimated. VERIFY vs GGUF once 24-32GB hardware is available.

offload_split_seed.rationale: Full residency expected at 24GB+ (all layers on GPU, no spill); leave null until measured on real 24-32GB hardware. The fit calculator will confirm full offload per machine.

ultra.agent_mode_future_note: v1 stays 'single' all tiers; this tier (24GB+) is where 'concurrent' (a co-resident agent model alongside the resident 30B) activates once built.
ultra._verify: Tier not yet validated on hardware; residency + context values provisional.

max.status: FUTURE / designed -- no 32GB+ hardware to validate yet; the 'bigger-than-30B' target
max.meaning: workstation / flagship hardware: larger-than-30B models (70B-class dense or a larger MoE), fully resident, longest context, headroom for concurrent agents

context.rationale: Provisional 128K @ q8_0; a 32GB+ card holds a large resident context easily. Settle against the chosen larger model's GGUF when built.

offload_split_seed.rationale: Full residency expected at 32GB+; leave null until a real model + machine are chosen. The fit calculator confirms per machine.

max.agent_mode_future_note: v1 stays 'single' all tiers; concurrent co-resident agents apply here too once built.
max._verify: Tier not yet validated on hardware; default model unchosen, all values provisional.

detection_to_tier: Pick tier by detected VRAM (half-open bands [lo, hi): vram >= lo and vram < hi), then verify RAM clears that tier's hard floor; if not, downgrade a tier or warn. Manual override always available in Settings (user-control).
